summary: @doaction/types preinstall hook exfiltrates environment variables to Datadog via transitive dependency
details: The package is published as 'Type definitions' but ships no type definitions. Its package.json declares a preinstall hook (`node scripts/postinstall.js`) whose body unconditionally calls `require('@doaction/shared/bin/preinstall.js')`. The shared dependency's preinstall code collects the installer's environment variables and POSTs them to a Datadog endpoint, and the package's own src/index.js re-exports only `collectEnv`, `sendToDatadog`, and `reportTypesEnv` — confirming the telemetry purpose. The behavior fires automatically on every `npm install` with no opt-in and no disclosure consistent with the advertised 'types' purpose. Installer environment variables routinely contain credentials (CI tokens, cloud keys, npm auth tokens), so unconsented install-time exfiltration of `process.env` to a third-party ingestion endpoint is a credential-disclosure risk regardless of the operator's intent. The misleading 'types' framing further indicates the telemetry is intended to run against installers who have no reason to expect network I/O from a type-definitions package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
